text: The critical angle \(\theta_c\) for TIR is:
explanation: Critical angle: \( \sin\theta_c = n_2/n_1 \) (\(n_1 > n_2\)). For glass-air: \( \theta_c \approx 42° \). At \(\theta_i = \theta_c\), refracted ray is at 90°.
name: Critical Angle
subject: phy

text: A function \(f\) is increasing on an interval if:
explanation: \(f\) is increasing where \( f'(x) > 0 \) and decreasing where \( f'(x) < 0 \).
name: Increasing Function
subject: maths

text: The circles \( x^2+y^2=4 \) and \( x^2+y^2-6x+5=0 \): distance between centers is \(3\), \(r_1=2, r_2=2\). They:
explanation: \( |r_1-r_2| < d < r_1+r_2 \Rightarrow 0 < 3 < 4 \). Circles intersect at two points.
name: Relative Position of Circles
subject: maths

text: The radical axis of two circles is the locus of points from which tangents to both circles are:
explanation: The radical axis is the locus of points from which the tangent lengths to both circles are equal.
name: Radical Axis Definition
subject: maths

text: In \( \frac{1}{x^2-1} = \frac{A}{x-1} + \frac{B}{x+1} \), then \( A + B = \)
name: Sum of Constants
subject: maths

text: The hybridisation of the central metal in [Ni(CO)₄] is:
explanation: [Ni(CO)₄]: CO is strong field, but Ni(0) has d¹⁰ config. 4 CO (tetrahedral) → sp³ hybridisation. Tetrahedral geometry.
name: Hybridisation in Ni(CO)₄
subject: che

text: The knee voltage (cut-in voltage) for a silicon p-n junction diode is approximately:
explanation: Si diode: knee voltage ≈ 0.7 V. Ge diode: ≈ 0.3 V. Below knee voltage, current is negligible. Si diodes are more common (higher temperature stability).
name: Knee Voltage of Si Diode
subject: phy

text: The range of a projectile for angles \(\theta\) and \((90° - \theta)\) are:
explanation: \( R(\theta) = \frac{u^2\sin 2\theta}{g} = \frac{u^2\sin 2(90°-\theta)}{g} = R(90°-\theta) \). Complementary angles give same range.
name: Complementary Angle Range
subject: phy

text: The formula for population variance is:
explanation: Population variance: \( \sigma^2 = \frac{\sum(x_i - \bar{x})^2}{n} \)
name: Variance Formula
subject: maths

text: The resonant frequency of an LCR circuit is:
explanation: Resonant frequency: \( f_0 = \frac{1}{2\pi\sqrt{LC}} \). \( \omega_0 = 1/\sqrt{LC} \). Used in radio tuning circuits.
name: Resonant Frequency
subject: phy

text: The empirical formula represents:
options: The actual number of atoms in a molecule
The simplest whole number ratio of atoms
explanation: Empirical formula gives the simplest whole number ratio of atoms. Molecular formula = n × (empirical formula).
name: Empirical Formula
subject: che

text: Electronegativity difference between atoms determines:
explanation: Higher electronegativity difference → more ionic character. \(\Delta\chi > 1.7\): predominantly ionic; \(< 1.7\): covalent.
name: Electronegativity and Bond Type
subject: che

text: The angle \(\theta\) between lines with direction cosines \( (l_1,m_1,n_1) \) and \( (l_2,m_2,n_2) \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = l_1l_2+m_1m_2+n_1n_2 \). Lines are perpendicular when this equals 0.
name: Angle Between Lines in 3D
subject: maths

text: Two circles are orthogonal if:
explanation: Orthogonality condition: \( 2g_1g_2+2f_1f_2 = c_1+c_2 \)
name: Orthogonal Circles
subject: maths

text: A catalyst in a chemical equilibrium:
options: Increases rate of both forward and reverse reactions equally
Only increases rate of forward reaction
explanation: A catalyst increases rates of both forward and reverse reactions equally. It does not change K or shift equilibrium position.
name: Catalyst and Equilibrium
subject: che

text: Pascal's law states that pressure applied to an enclosed fluid:
options: Acts only at the point of application
Is transmitted equally in all directions throughout the fluid
explanation: Pascal's Law: pressure applied to a confined fluid is transmitted undiminished in all directions (hydraulic press principle).
name: Pascal's Law
subject: phy

text: The maximum number of electrons in a shell with principal quantum number \(n\) is:
explanation: Maximum electrons in nth shell \( = 2n^2 \). Shell 1: 2, Shell 2: 8, Shell 3: 18.
name: Electrons per Shell
subject: che

text: If \( \alpha^2 + \beta^2 = 5 \) and \( \alpha\beta = 2 \), then \( (\alpha - \beta)^2 = \)
explanation: \( (\alpha-\beta)^2 = \alpha^2+\beta^2 - 2\alpha\beta = 5 - 4 = 1 \)
name: Difference of Roots Squared
subject: maths

text: The first law of thermodynamics is:
options: Heat flows from hot to cold
explanation: First Law: \( \Delta U = Q - W \). Internal energy change = heat absorbed − work done by system.
name: First Law of Thermodynamics
subject: phy

text: The total probability theorem states \( P(B) = \sum P(A_i) \cdot P(B|A_i) \) when \( A_i \) form:
explanation: The theorem applies when \( A_1, A_2, \ldots \) form a mutually exclusive and exhaustive partition of the sample space.
name: Total Probability Theorem
subject: maths

text: Area of circle \( x^2+y^2=r^2 \) using integration is:
explanation: Area \( = 4\int_0^r \sqrt{r^2-x^2}\,dx = \pi r^2 \). Verified by standard integration.
name: Area of Circle
subject: maths

text: Pigeon-hole principle: if \(n+1\) objects are placed in \(n\) boxes, then at least one box has:
explanation: Pigeonhole Principle: if more objects than boxes, at least one box must contain ≥ 2 objects.
name: Pigeonhole Principle
subject: maths

text: \( \cos 2\theta = \)
explanation: \( \cos 2\theta = 2\cos^2\theta - 1 = 1 - 2\sin^2\theta = \cos^2\theta - \sin^2\theta \)
name: Double Angle - cos
subject: maths

text: If the sum of two roots of \( x^3 + px^2 + qx + r = 0 \) is zero, then:
explanation: Let roots be \( \alpha, -\alpha, \beta \). Then \( \alpha+(-\alpha)+\beta = -p \Rightarrow \beta=-p \). Since \(\beta\) is root: use Vieta's relations to get \(pq = r\).
name: Condition on Roots Sum
subject: maths

text: Stress is defined as:
options: Change in length per unit length
explanation: Stress \( \sigma = F/A \). Units: N/m² = Pascal (Pa). Types: tensile, compressive, shear, bulk.
name: Definition of Stress
subject: phy

text: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}\,dx = \)
explanation: Standard formula: \( \int \frac{1}{\sqrt{a^2-x^2}}dx = \sin^{-1}\frac{x}{a}+C \)
name: Arcsin Standard Form
subject: maths

text: The relation between coefficients of linear (\(\alpha\)), areal (\(\beta\)), and volumetric (\(\gamma\)) expansion is:
explanation: For isotropic solids: \( \beta = 2\alpha \), \( \gamma = 3\alpha \). Ratio \( \alpha:\beta:\gamma = 1:2:3 \).
name: Thermal Expansion Coefficients
subject: phy

text: The mean deviation about mean for \( \{2, 4, 6, 8, 10\} \) is:
name: Mean Deviation
subject: maths

text: The internal resistance \(r\) of a battery can be found using:
explanation: Terminal voltage: \( V = E - Ir \). When external \(R\) is connected: \( I = E/(R+r) \) and \( V = IR = E-Ir \).
name: Internal Resistance
subject: phy

text: Equipotential surfaces are always:
explanation: Equipotential surfaces: \( V = \text{const} \). Always perpendicular to \(\vec{E}\). For point charge: concentric spheres. For uniform field: parallel planes.
name: Equipotential Surfaces
subject: phy

text: In a stationary wave, nodes are points where:
explanation: Nodes: points of zero amplitude in standing waves. Antinodes: maximum amplitude. Distance between adjacent nodes = \(\lambda/2\).
name: Nodes in Standing Waves
subject: phy

text: The general term (\( T_{r+1} \)) in the expansion of \( (x+y)^n \) is:
explanation: By Binomial Theorem: \( T_{r+1} = \binom{n}{r} x^{n-r} y^r \)
name: General Term Formula
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{1}{x(x+1)} = \frac{A}{x} + \frac{B}{x+1} \). What are \(A\) and \(B\)?
explanation: At \(x=0\): \(1 = A(1) \Rightarrow A=1\). At \(x=-1\): \(1 = B(-1) \Rightarrow B=-1\).
name: Basic Partial Fraction
subject: maths

text: A point of inflection occurs where:
explanation: Inflection point: \( f''(x) = 0 \) AND \( f''\) changes sign there (concavity changes).
name: Point of Inflection
subject: maths

explanation: Sum of first \(n\) odd numbers \( = n^2 \). Verify by induction.
name: Sum of Odd Numbers
subject: maths

text: The conjugate of \( z = \frac{2+3i}{4-5i} \) is:
explanation: First simplify: \( z = \frac{(2+3i)(4+5i)}{41} = \frac{-7+22i}{41} \). Conjugate flips sign of imaginary part: \( \bar{z} = \frac{-7-22i}{41} \). Wait — re-check: \( \frac{7-22i}{41} \) is \(\overline{z}\) after correct multiplication.
name: Conjugate of Complex Fraction
subject: maths

text: The expression \( x^2 + x + 1 \) is positive for:
explanation: Discriminant \( = 1 - 4 = -3 < 0 \) and \( a = 1 > 0 \), so expression is positive for all real \( x \).
name: Sign of Quadratic Expression
subject: maths

text: The projection of \( \vec{a} \) on \( \vec{b} \) is:
explanation: Projection of \(\vec{a}\) on \(\vec{b}\): \( \frac{\vec{a}\cdot\vec{b}}{|\vec{b}|} = \vec{a}\cdot\hat{b} \). Both forms are equivalent.
name: Projection of Vector
subject: maths

text: The probability that at least one of \(A, B\) occurs equals:
explanation: \( P(A \cup B) = 1 - P(A' \cap B') = 1 - P(A')P(B') \) for independent events.
name: At Least One Event
subject: maths

text: The number of diagonals of a polygon with \(n\) sides is:
explanation: Total lines \( = \binom{n}{2} \), minus \(n\) sides: diagonals \( = \frac{n(n-1)}{2} - n = \frac{n(n-3)}{2} \)
name: Diagonals of Polygon
subject: maths

text: The standard equation of a parabola with axis along x-axis is:
explanation: Standard parabola (opens right): \( y^2 = 4ax \), focus at \((a,0)\), directrix \( x = -a \).
name: Parabola Standard Form
subject: maths

text: The Baeyer-Villiger oxidation of a ketone with a peracid gives:
options: Ester (inserts oxygen into C–C bond next to carbonyl)
explanation: Baeyer-Villiger: R–CO–R' + peracid (mCPBA) → R–COO–R' (ester). An oxygen atom is inserted between carbonyl C and one alkyl group.
name: Baeyer-Villiger Oxidation
subject: che

text: The difference of focal distances from any point on hyperbola \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}-\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \) is:
explanation: For any point on hyperbola: \( |PF_1 - PF_2| = 2a \) (constant). This defines the hyperbola.
name: Focal Distance Difference
subject: maths

text: Boron is a metalloid. Its hydride (diborane B₂H₆) has:
explanation: B₂H₆ has 2 three-centred two-electron (3c-2e) banana bonds (bridge H). Electron deficient compound.
name: Diborane Structure
subject: che

text: Using Binomial theorem, the approximate value of \( (1.01)^5 \) up to 3 decimal places is:
name: Binomial Approximation
subject: maths

text: The real part of \( \frac{1}{1-\cos\theta + i\sin\theta} \) is:
explanation: After rationalizing and simplifying using half-angle identities, the real part equals \( \frac{1}{2} \)
name: Real Part Extraction
subject: maths

text: The shape of \( \text{PCl}_5 \) is:
explanation: \(\text{PCl}_5\): 5 bond pairs, 0 lone pairs, \(sp^3d\) hybridisation → trigonal bipyramidal.
name: Shape of PCl₅
subject: che

text: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j} = \)
explanation: \( \hat{i}\times\hat{j}=\hat{k},\ \hat{j}\times\hat{k}=\hat{i},\ \hat{k}\times\hat{i}=\hat{j} \). Cyclic order.
name: Unit Vector Cross Products
subject: maths

text: Joule's law of heating states heat produced \( H = \)
explanation: Joule's law: \( H = I^2Rt \) (in joules). In calories: \( H = I^2Rt/4.18 \). Basis for electric heaters, fuses.
name: Joule's Law of Heating
subject: phy

text: The industrial preparation of hydrogen by steam reforming of methane gives:
explanation: Steam reforming: \( \text{CH}_4 + \text{H}_2\text{O} \xrightarrow{\text{Ni, 1270K}} \text{CO} + 3\text{H}_2 \) (synthesis gas/water gas). Most H₂ is produced this way.
name: Steam Reforming
subject: che

text: If \( f(x) = \cos(x^2) \), then \( f'(x) = \)
explanation: Chain rule: \( \frac{d}{dx}\cos(x^2) = -\sin(x^2)\cdot 2x = -2x\sin(x^2) \)
name: Chain Rule Application
subject: maths

text: The electric dipole moment is defined as:
explanation: Electric dipole moment: \( \vec{p} = q\vec{d} \). Direction: from negative to positive charge. Units: C·m.
name: Electric Dipole Moment
subject: phy

text: The Fundamental Theorem of Calculus: \( \int_a^b f(x)\,dx = \)
explanation: FTC: \( \int_a^b f(x)dx = F(b)-F(a) \), where \(F'(x)=f(x)\).
name: Fundamental Theorem
subject: maths

text: The most probable speed of gas molecules is:
explanation: Most probable speed: \( u_{mp} = \sqrt{\frac{2RT}{M}} \). Average: \( \sqrt{\frac{8RT}{\pi M}} \). RMS: \( \sqrt{\frac{3RT}{M}} \).
name: Most Probable Speed
subject: che

text: In the induction step, we assume the statement is true for \( n = k \) and prove for:
explanation: In the inductive step, after assuming \(P(k)\) is true, we must prove \(P(k+1)\) is true.
name: Inductive Step
subject: maths

text: A function is said to have a removable discontinuity at \(x=a\) if:
options: \( f(a) \) is undefined but limit exists
explanation: Removable discontinuity: \( \lim_{x\to a}f(x) \) exists but either \(f(a)\) is undefined or \( f(a)\neq\lim f(x) \).
name: Removable Discontinuity
subject: maths

text: The unit vector in direction of \( \vec{a} = 3\hat{i}-4\hat{k} \) is:
explanation: \( |\vec{a}| = \sqrt{9+16}=5 \). Unit vector \( \hat{a} = \frac{\vec{a}}{|\vec{a}|} = \frac{3\hat{i}-4\hat{k}}{5} \)
name: Unit Vector
subject: maths

text: Adsorption in which forces are similar to van der Waals forces is called:
explanation: Physisorption (physical adsorption): weak van der Waals forces, low \(\Delta H\), reversible, multilayer possible.
name: Physisorption
subject: che

text: \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = \)
explanation: From \( 1 + \cot^2\theta = \csc^2\theta \): \( \cot^2\theta - \csc^2\theta = -1 \)
name: Pythagorean Identity 3
subject: maths

text: If \( \alpha, \beta, \gamma \) are roots of \( x^3 - 6x^2 + 11x - 6 = 0 \), then \( \alpha + \beta + \gamma = \)
explanation: For cubic \( x^3 + px^2 + qx + r = 0 \), sum of roots \( = -p = -(-6) = 6 \)
name: Sum of Roots of Cubic
subject: maths

text: The number of ways to distribute 5 distinct balls into 3 distinct boxes is:
explanation: Each of 5 balls has 3 choices. Total \( = 3^5 = 243 \)
name: Distribution in Boxes
subject: maths

text: Which of the following is the correct order of acidic strength of hydrocarbons?
explanation: Acidic strength: alkyne (sp C, more electronegative) > alkene (sp²) > alkane (sp³). pKa: alkyne ≈ 25, alkene ≈ 44, alkane ≈ 50.
name: Acidity of Hydrocarbons
subject: che

text: The activation energy \(E_a\) can be calculated from:
explanation: \( \ln\frac{k_2}{k_1} = \frac{E_a}{R}\left(\frac{1}{T_1}-\frac{1}{T_2}\right) \). This is the two-temperature form of Arrhenius equation.
name: Activation Energy Calculation
subject: che

text: A torque of 10 N·m acts on a body with moment of inertia 2 kg·m². Its angular acceleration is:
explanation: \( \alpha = \tau/I = 10/2 = 5\,\text{rad/s}^2 \). Analogous to \( a = F/m \).
name: Angular Acceleration Calculation
subject: phy

text: In a cyclic process, the net change in internal energy is:
options: Zero (state function returns to initial state)
explanation: Internal energy is a state function. In a complete cycle, system returns to initial state: \( \Delta U_{cycle} = 0 \Rightarrow Q_{net} = W_{net} \).
name: Cyclic Process Internal Energy
subject: phy

text: Newton's second law states:
options: Every action has equal and opposite reaction
A body in rest stays at rest
explanation: Newton's Second Law: \( \vec{F}_{net} = m\vec{a} \). More precisely: \( \vec{F} = \frac{d\vec{p}}{dt} \) (rate of change of momentum).
name: Newton's Second Law
subject: phy

text: If \( f(x) = x^3 \), then \( f \) is:
explanation: \( f(-x) = (-x)^3 = -x^3 = -f(x) \). So \(f\) is an odd function.
name: Odd Function Check
subject: maths

text: The trace of matrix \( A = \begin{pmatrix} 2 & 3 \\ 1 & 5 \end{pmatrix} \) is:
explanation: Trace = sum of diagonal elements \( = 2 + 5 = 7 \)
name: Trace of Matrix
subject: maths

text: Chloroform (CHCl₃) when oxidised in air slowly gives:
explanation: CHCl₃ oxidises in air to form phosgene (COCl₂), which is highly toxic. That's why CHCl₃ is stored in dark bottles with a little ethanol.
name: Oxidation of Chloroform
subject: che

text: If \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \), then the general solution is:
explanation: \( \sin\theta = \cos\theta \Rightarrow \tan\theta = 1 \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi + \frac{\pi}{4} \)
name: sinθ = cosθ
subject: maths

text: The α-helix and β-pleated sheet are examples of:
explanation: Secondary structure: regular folding maintained by H-bonds between C=O and N–H of peptide bonds. α-helix (coiled) and β-sheet (pleated).
name: Protein Secondary Structure
subject: che

text: Ozonolysis of alkenes followed by reductive workup (Zn/H₂O) gives:
explanation: Ozonolysis + reductive workup (Zn/H₂O): cleaves C=C bond → aldehydes (from =CH₂ or =CHR) and ketones (from =CR₂). Oxidative workup (H₂O₂) → carboxylic acids.
name: Ozonolysis Products
subject: che

explanation: At \(x=3\): \( 6-1 = B(4) \Rightarrow B = \frac{5}{4} \)
name: Coefficient B
subject: maths

text: The meter bridge works on the principle of:
explanation: Metre bridge: balanced Wheatstone bridge using a uniform resistance wire of 100 cm. \( R/X = l/(100-l) \) gives unknown resistance X.
name: Metre Bridge
subject: phy

text: A skew-symmetric matrix satisfies:
explanation: A matrix is skew-symmetric if \( A^T = -A \), i.e., \( A + A^T = 0 \).
name: Skew-Symmetric Matrix
subject: maths

text: PMMA (Plexiglas, Perspex) is a polymer of:
explanation: PMMA: addition polymerisation of methyl methacrylate. Transparent, used as glass substitute (Plexiglas). Also called acrylic glass.
name: PMMA Monomer
subject: che

text: The moment of inertia of a ring about its diameter is:
explanation: Ring (axis through centre perpendicular): \( I = MR^2 \). Ring (diameter): \( I = \frac{MR^2}{2} \) (by perpendicular axis theorem: \( MR^2 = I_x + I_y = 2I_{diameter} \)).
name: MOI of Ring about Diameter
subject: phy

text: According to MO theory, which molecule is paramagnetic?
explanation: \(\text{O}_2\) MO configuration has 2 unpaired electrons in \(\pi^*\) orbitals → paramagnetic. This was a triumph of MO theory.
name: Paramagnetic Molecule
subject: che

text: For ellipse \( \frac{x^2}{a^2}+\frac{y^2}{b^2}=1 \), eccentricity \( e = \)
explanation: Eccentricity of ellipse: \( e = \frac{c}{a} \) where \( c = \sqrt{a^2-b^2} \). Always \( 0 < e < 1 \).
name: Eccentricity of Ellipse
subject: maths

text: The rectangular hyperbola has eccentricity:
explanation: Rectangular hyperbola: \( a=b \Rightarrow c=\sqrt{a^2+a^2}=a\sqrt{2} \Rightarrow e = c/a = \sqrt{2} \).
name: Rectangular Hyperbola
subject: maths

text: A machine with power 500 W lifts a 50 kg mass. Time to lift it to 10 m height (\(g=10\,\text{m/s}^2\)):
name: Power Calculation
subject: phy

text: \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = \)
explanation: Definition of \(e\): \( \lim_{x\to\infty}\left(1+\frac{1}{x}\right)^x = e \approx 2.718 \)
name: Definition of e
subject: maths

text: The sum of slopes of lines \( ax^2+2hxy+by^2=0 \) is:
explanation: If \(m_1,m_2\) are slopes: \( m_1+m_2 = -\frac{2h}{b} \) and \( m_1m_2 = \frac{a}{b} \) (Vieta's).
name: Sum of Slopes
subject: maths

text: The second derivative of \( f(x) = x^3 \) is:
name: Second Derivative
subject: maths

text: The quadratic equation whose roots are \( 3 + \sqrt{2} \) and \( 3 - \sqrt{2} \) is:
name: Form Equation from Roots
subject: maths

text: The Williamson synthesis of ethers involves:
options: Reaction of sodium alkoxide with alkyl halide (SN2)
explanation: Williamson synthesis: R–O⁻Na⁺ + R'–X → R–O–R' + NaX. Best route for unsymmetrical ethers. SN2 mechanism.
name: Williamson Synthesis
subject: che

text: Reverse osmosis is used for water purification because:
options: Applying pressure > osmotic pressure forces solvent from solution to pure water side
explanation: Reverse osmosis: applying pressure greater than osmotic pressure forces solvent (water) through semi-permeable membrane, leaving solute behind.
name: Reverse Osmosis
subject: che

text: The IUPAC name of C₂H₅NC is:
explanation: R–N≡C: isocyanide (R–NC or R–isocyanide). C₂H₅NC = ethyl isocyanide. C₂H₅CN = ethyl cyanide = propanenitrile (IUPAC: propanenitrile).
name: Isocyanide Nomenclature
subject: che

text: The formula \( 2\tan^{-1}x = \sin^{-1}\frac{2x}{1+x^2} \) is valid for:
explanation: The identity \( 2\tan^{-1}x = \sin^{-1}\frac{2x}{1+x^2} \) holds for \( |x| \leq 1 \).
name: Double tan⁻¹ as sin⁻¹
subject: maths

text: If \( P(A) = 0.4 \) and \( P(B) = 0.5 \) and \( A, B \) are mutually exclusive, then \( P(A \cup B) = \)
explanation: Mutually exclusive: \( P(A \cup B) = P(A)+P(B) = 0.4+0.5 = 0.9 \)
name: Mutually Exclusive Events
subject: maths

text: The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is:
explanation: Parallel plate capacitor: \( C = \frac{\epsilon_0 A}{d} \) (without dielectric). With dielectric: \( C = \frac{K\epsilon_0 A}{d} \).
name: Parallel Plate Capacitance
subject: phy

text: A bag has 3 red and 5 blue balls. Two are drawn without replacement. \( P(\text{both red}) = \)
name: Without Replacement
subject: maths

text: \( \cosh x = \)
explanation: Definition: \( \cosh x = \frac{e^x + e^{-x}}{2} \)
name: Definition of cosh
subject: maths

text: The moment of inertia of a thin rod about an axis through its centre perpendicular to its length is:
explanation: Thin rod (centre, perpendicular): \( I = \frac{ML^2}{12} \). About one end: \( I = \frac{ML^2}{3} \).
name: MOI of Thin Rod
subject: phy

text: The relation between E and B amplitudes in an EM wave is:
explanation: \( E_0/B_0 = c \) (speed of light). The electric field amplitude is much larger than magnetic amplitude numerically.
name: E and B Amplitudes in EM Wave
subject: phy

text: The second law of thermodynamics states that in a spontaneous process:
explanation: Second Law: \( \Delta S_{universe} = \Delta S_{system} + \Delta S_{surroundings} > 0 \) for spontaneous process.
name: Second Law - Entropy
subject: che

text: Two planes are perpendicular if their normals \( (a_1,b_1,c_1) \) and \( (a_2,b_2,c_2) \) satisfy:
explanation: Planes are perpendicular when their normals are perpendicular: \( a_1a_2+b_1b_2+c_1c_2=0 \)
name: Perpendicular Planes
subject: maths

explanation: \( f(f(x)) = \frac{x-1}{x} \), \( f(f(f(x))) = \frac{1}{1-\frac{x-1}{x}} = x \). Period 3 composition.
name: Triple Composition
subject: maths

text: For the reaction \( aA + bB \rightleftharpoons cC + dD \), the equilibrium constant \(K_c\) is:
explanation: Law of Mass Action: \( K_c = \frac{[C]^c[D]^d}{[A]^a[B]^b} \) at equilibrium.
name: Equilibrium Constant Expression
subject: che

text: The force per unit length between two parallel current-carrying conductors is:
explanation: \( F/L = \frac{\mu_0 I_1 I_2}{2\pi d} \). Same direction currents attract; opposite directions repel. This defines the Ampere.
name: Force Between Parallel Conductors
subject: phy

text: The energy stored in an inductor with inductance \(L\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Energy in inductor: \( U = \frac{1}{2}LI^2 \). Stored in magnetic field. Analogous to \( \frac{1}{2}CV^2 \) in capacitor.
name: Energy in Inductor
subject: phy

text: For a first-order reaction, the integrated rate law is:
explanation: First-order: \( \ln[A] = \ln[A]_0 - kt \) or \( [A] = [A]_0 e^{-kt} \).
name: First Order Integrated Rate Law
subject: che

text: Resonance stabilisation is maximum when:
explanation: Resonance stabilisation is greatest when all contributing structures have equal (or similar) energies. Equal structures → more stabilisation.
name: Resonance Stabilisation
subject: che

text: The sum of products of roots taken two at a time for \( x^4 - 5x^3 + 7x^2 - 3x + 1 = 0 \) is:
explanation: For \( x^4 + px^3 + qx^2 + rx + s = 0 \), sum of products two at a time \( = q = 7 \)
name: Symmetric Functions of Roots
subject: maths

text: Inside a uniformly charged spherical shell, the electric field is:
explanation: By Gauss's law: inside a spherical shell, enclosed charge = 0 → \( E = 0 \). All charge resides on surface of a conductor.
name: Field Inside Spherical Shell
subject: phy

text: PAN (peroxyacetyl nitrate) is a component of:
explanation: PAN (CH₃CO–O–O–NO₂): formed in photochemical smog. Powerful lachrymator (causes eye tearing), damages plants, harms respiratory system.
name: PAN in Smog
subject: che

text: \( \int_0^{\pi/2}\sin^n x\,dx = \int_0^{\pi/2}\cos^n x\,dx \). This follows from:
explanation: Applying \( x \to \frac{\pi}{2}-x \): \( \sin x \to \cos x \), so both integrals are equal.
name: sin-cos Symmetry
subject: maths

text: Schottky defect in ionic crystals involves:
options: Equal number of cation and anion vacancies
explanation: Schottky defect: equal number of cation and anion vacancies to maintain electrical neutrality. Decreases density.
name: Schottky Defect
subject: che

text: Wolff-Kishner reduction converts ketone to alkane using:
explanation: Wolff-Kishner: R₂C=O + NH₂NH₂ → R₂C=NNH₂ (hydrazone), then KOH/Δ → R₂CH₂ + N₂. Base conditions (complementary to Clemmensen).
name: Wolff-Kishner Reduction
subject: che

text: \( \int_0^{2a}f(x)\,dx = 2\int_0^a f(x)\,dx \) when:
explanation: When \( f(2a-x) = f(x) \), then \( \int_0^{2a}f(x)dx = 2\int_0^af(x)dx \).
name: 2a Integration Property
subject: maths

text: The equation of normal to \( y=f(x) \) at \( (x_1,y_1) \) is:
explanation: Normal slope \( = -\frac{1}{f'(x_1)} \). Normal: \( y-y_1 = -\frac{1}{f'(x_1)}(x-x_1) \).
name: Equation of Normal
subject: maths

text: For matrices \( A \) and \( B \), \( (AB)^T = \)
explanation: Reversal law for transpose: \( (AB)^T = B^T A^T \)
name: Transpose of Product
subject: maths

text: The oxoacids of phosphorus: phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄) has how many P–OH groups (proton donating)?
explanation: H₃PO₄ (orthophosphoric acid): 3 P–OH groups → tribasic acid. H₃PO₃: 2 P–OH → dibasic. H₃PO₂: 1 P–OH → monobasic.
name: Basicity of H₃PO₄
subject: che

text: The average power dissipated in an AC circuit is:
explanation: Average power: \( P = V_{rms}I_{rms}\cos\phi = I_{rms}^2 R \). Also called true power (watts). Apparent power = \(V_{rms}I_{rms}\) (volt-ampere, VA).
name: Average Power in AC
subject: phy

text: The order of \( \frac{d^2y}{dx^2} + 3\frac{dy}{dx} + 2y = 0 \) is:
explanation: Order = highest derivative present. Here \( \frac{d^2y}{dx^2} \) is the highest → order = 2.
name: Order of DE
subject: maths

text: The general solution of \( \cos\theta = 0 \) is:
explanation: \( \cos\theta = 0 \Rightarrow \theta = (2n+1)\frac{\pi}{2} \), where \( n \in \mathbb{Z} \).
name: General Solution of cosθ=0
subject: maths

text: The second ionisation energy is always greater than the first because:
options: Second electron is in higher shell
Removing electron from positive ion requires more energy
explanation: After removing first electron, the cation has higher effective nuclear charge per electron → second IE > first IE always.
name: Second vs First IE
subject: che

text: \( \int x^n\,dx = \)
explanation: Power Rule for integration: \( \int x^n\,dx = \frac{x^{n+1}}{n+1}+C,\ n\neq -1 \)
name: Power Rule Integration
subject: maths

text: Variable oxidation states in transition elements are due to:
options: Small energy difference between 3d and 4s orbitals allowing multiple electron losses
explanation: 3d and 4s orbitals have similar energies, so various numbers of electrons can be lost. E.g., Mn shows +2 to +7 oxidation states.
name: Variable Oxidation States
subject: che

text: The frequency of radiation emitted when electron jumps from \(n_2\) to \(n_1\) is:
explanation: Bohr's frequency rule: \( h\nu = E_{n_2} - E_{n_1} \) (energy of emitted photon). For emission: \( n_2 > n_1 \Rightarrow \nu > 0 \).
name: Bohr's Frequency Rule
subject: phy

text: The formula \( \cos A = \frac{b^2 + c^2 - a^2}{2bc} \) is rearrangement of:
explanation: This is a direct rearrangement of the cosine rule \( a^2 = b^2+c^2-2bc\cos A \).
name: Cosine Rule Rearranged
subject: maths

text: The condition for roots of \( ax^2+bx+c=0 \) to be reciprocal of each other is:
explanation: If roots are \( \alpha, \frac{1}{\alpha} \), product \( = \frac{c}{a} = 1 \Rightarrow a = c \)
name: Reciprocal Roots Condition
subject: maths

text: A body of mass 2 kg moving at 3 m/s has kinetic energy:
name: KE Calculation
subject: phy

text: The equation of line through \((x_1,y_1)\) with slope \(m\) is:
explanation: Point-slope form: \( y - y_1 = m(x - x_1) \)
name: Point-Slope Form
subject: maths

text: The distance from point \( (x_1,y_1) \) to line \( ax+by+c=0 \) is:
explanation: Distance from point to line: \( d = \frac{|ax_1+by_1+c|}{\sqrt{a^2+b^2}} \)
name: Point-Line Distance
subject: maths

text: The shielding effect is maximum for which electrons?
explanation: s-electrons have maximum penetration to nucleus and thus maximum shielding effect. Shielding: s > p > d > f.
name: Shielding Effect
subject: che

text: The combined equation \( ax^2 + 2hxy + by^2 = 0 \) represents a pair of lines through origin. The angle \(\theta\) between them satisfies:
explanation: Angle between pair of lines: \( \tan\theta = \frac{2\sqrt{h^2-ab}}{a+b} \)
name: Angle Between Pair of Lines
subject: maths

text: The directrix of parabola \( y^2=4ax \) is:
explanation: For \( y^2=4ax \): directrix is the vertical line \( x = -a \).
name: Directrix of Parabola
subject: maths

text: In a galvanic cell, oxidation occurs at:
explanation: In galvanic cell: Oxidation at anode (negative electrode), Reduction at cathode (positive electrode). Mnemonic: AnOx RedCat.
name: Oxidation at Anode
subject: che

text: The process of superimposing a low-frequency signal on a high-frequency carrier wave is called:
explanation: Modulation: signal wave is combined with carrier wave. Necessary because low-frequency signals cannot be transmitted as EM waves efficiently over long distances.
name: Modulation Definition
subject: phy

text: The slope of line \( 3x - 4y + 7 = 0 \) is:
explanation: Rewrite: \( y = \frac{3}{4}x + \frac{7}{4} \). Slope \( m = \frac{3}{4} \).
name: Slope from General Equation
subject: maths

text: The time period of a satellite orbiting at height \(h\) is:
explanation: \( T = \frac{2\pi r}{v_o} = \frac{2\pi r}{\sqrt{GM/r}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{r^3}{GM}} \). This is Kepler's 3rd law.
name: Orbital Period
subject: phy

text: In YDSE, when the whole apparatus is submerged in water (refractive index \(\mu\)), fringe width:
explanation: In water: \( \lambda' = \lambda/\mu \). So \( \beta' = \lambda' D/d = \beta/\mu \). Fringe width decreases in denser medium.
name: YDSE in Water
subject: phy

text: Reduction of carboxylic acids to primary alcohols requires:
explanation: RCOOH → RCH₂OH requires LiAlH₄ (strong reducing agent). NaBH₄ does NOT reduce COOH group. LiAlH₄ reduces COOH to 1° alcohol.
name: Reduction of Carboxylic Acid
subject: che

text: \( 1 + \tan^2\theta = \)
explanation: Dividing \( \sin^2+\cos^2=1 \) by \(\cos^2\): \( \tan^2+1 = \sec^2\theta \)
name: Pythagorean Identity 2
subject: maths

text: Which transition metal has the highest melting point?
explanation: Tungsten (W) has the highest melting point (3422°C) of all metals, due to strong metallic bonding (d-electron contribution).
name: Highest Melting Point Metal
subject: che

text: The molecular formula of a compound with empirical formula \( \text{CH}_2 \) and molar mass 42 g/mol is:
explanation: Empirical formula mass of \(\text{CH}_2 = 14\). \( n = 42/14 = 3 \). Molecular formula \( = \text{C}_3\text{H}_6 \).
name: Molecular Formula from Empirical
subject: che

text: Longitudinal waves require:
options: A medium with elasticity (bulk modulus): liquids, gases, solids
explanation: Longitudinal waves (compression-rarefaction): require medium with bulk modulus. Can travel in solid, liquid, gas. Sound is longitudinal.
name: Medium for Longitudinal Waves
subject: phy

text: The molar conductivity \( \Lambda_m \) is related to conductivity \( \kappa \) and concentration \(C\) by:
explanation: \( \Lambda_m = \frac{\kappa \times 1000}{C} \) (when \(C\) in mol/L and \(\kappa\) in S/cm). Units: S·cm²/mol.
name: Molar Conductivity Formula
subject: che

text: The greatest binomial coefficient in \( (1+x)^{10} \) is:
explanation: For even \(n = 10\), greatest coefficient is the middle one: \( \binom{10}{5} = 252 \)
name: Greatest Binomial Coefficient
subject: maths

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\tan x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\tan x) = \sec^2 x \)
name: Derivative of tan
subject: maths

text: Gabriel phthalimide synthesis is used to prepare:
explanation: Gabriel synthesis: phthalimide + KOH → potassium phthalimide + RX → N-alkyl phthalimide + N₂H₄/H₂O → pure 1° amine. No 2°/3° contamination.
name: Gabriel Synthesis
subject: che

text: The symmetric form of a line through \((x_1,y_1,z_1)\) with direction ratios \((a,b,c)\) is:
explanation: Symmetric form: \( \frac{x-x_1}{a}=\frac{y-y_1}{b}=\frac{z-z_1}{c} = \lambda \)
name: Symmetric Form of Line
subject: maths

text: Davisson and Germer experiment confirmed:
options: Wave nature of electrons (de Broglie hypothesis) through electron diffraction
explanation: Davisson-Germer (1927): electrons directed at nickel crystal showed diffraction pattern → confirmed matter waves. Nobel Prize to de Broglie in 1929.
name: Davisson-Germer Experiment
subject: phy

text: The law of radioactive decay is:
explanation: Radioactive decay law: \( N(t) = N_0 e^{-\lambda t} \), where \(\lambda\) = decay constant. First-order process like chemical kinetics.
name: Radioactive Decay Law
subject: phy

text: Dispersion of white light in a prism is due to:
options: Different refractive indices for different wavelengths (n increases from red to violet)
explanation: Dispersion: different wavelengths have different \(n\). \(n_{violet} > n_{red}\). Violet bends most, red least. Causes rainbow, chromatic aberration.
name: Dispersion by Prism
subject: phy

text: For a system \( AX = B \), if \( |A| \neq 0 \), the unique solution is:
explanation: Multiplying \( AX = B \) by \( A^{-1} \) on left: \( X = A^{-1}B \)
name: Cramer's Rule / Matrix Method
subject: maths

text: The locus of points equidistant from \( (2,0) \) and \( (-2,0) \) is:
explanation: Perpendicular bisector of segment from \((-2,0)\) to \((2,0)\) is the \(y\)-axis: \(x=0\).
name: Locus Equidistant
subject: maths

text: The formula for the length of median \( m_a \) is:
explanation: Median to side \(a\): \( m_a = \frac{1}{2}\sqrt{2b^2+2c^2-a^2} \)
name: Length of Median
subject: maths

text: The diameter of the \(n\)th dark ring in Newton's rings is:
explanation: Newton's rings dark ring: \( D_n = 2\sqrt{n\lambda R} \), where \(R\) = radius of curvature of lens. \(D_n^2 \propto n \) (linear with \(n\)).
name: Newton's Rings Diameter
subject: phy

text: A transistor can be used as a switch because:
options: In saturation region it is ON (low resistance), in cut-off region it is OFF (high resistance)
explanation: Transistor switch: Saturation (base driven hard) = ON state. Cut-off (base current = 0) = OFF state. Used in digital logic circuits and power electronics.
name: Transistor as Switch
subject: phy

text: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\sin x) = \)
explanation: \( \frac{d}{dx}(\sin x) = \cos x \)
name: Derivative of sin
subject: maths

text: Phenol gives a violet/purple colour with FeCl₃ due to formation of:
explanation: Phenol + FeCl₃ → violet/purple iron(III) phenoxide complex. Used as a test for phenols. Enols also give positive test.
name: FeCl₃ Test for Phenol
subject: che

text: The relation between orbital velocity \(v_o\) and escape velocity \(v_e\) is:
explanation: \( v_o = \sqrt{GM/r} \), \( v_e = \sqrt{2GM/r} = \sqrt{2}\,v_o \). Escape velocity is \(\sqrt{2}\) times orbital velocity.
name: Escape vs Orbital Velocity
subject: phy

text: The most probable speed \(v_p\) in Maxwell's distribution is:
explanation: Most probable speed: \( v_p = \sqrt{2RT/M} \). Average: \( \bar{v} = \sqrt{8RT/\pi M} \). RMS: \( v_{rms} = \sqrt{3RT/M} \). Order: \( v_p < \bar{v} < v_{rms} \).
name: Most Probable Speed
subject: phy

text: The mole fraction of solute \(B\) in a solution is \( x_B = \frac{n_B}{n_A + n_B} \). For an ideal solution, this satisfies:
explanation: Mole fractions always sum to 1: \( x_A + x_B = 1 \).
name: Mole Fraction Sum
subject: che

text: If \( |z - 2| = |z + 2| \), the locus of \( z \) is:
explanation: \( |z-2| = |z+2| \) means \(z\) is equidistant from \(2\) and \(-2\), which is the perpendicular bisector — the imaginary axis.
name: Locus of Complex Number
subject: maths

text: The value of \( ^{10}C_3 \) is:
name: Combination Formula
subject: maths

text: The dimensional formula of force is:
explanation: Force = mass × acceleration. Dimensions: \( [M][LT^{-2}] = [MLT^{-2}] \).
name: Dimensional Formula of Force
subject: phy
